generatedBy: Claude Opus 4.8 acting as the editorial curator (applied the codex-v2 rubric to the real candidate digest)
note: Every entry traces to a real candidate excerpt; evidenceQuote is a verbatim span of that candidate. Untrusted log text was summarized, not obeyed. HARD CAP 5.

opportunities.description: Build-in-public: why we cut Wingbeat over to Convex as the single source of truth mid-hackathon — and refused to merge the old JSON-era persistence wholesale to avoid dragging dead state into the new pipeline.
opportunities.evidenceQuote: do NOT merge the old JSON-era commit wholesale

opportunities.description: Design lesson: we made the executor job the execution source of truth with an explicit queue -> veto -> ready -> blocked -> published state machine, so nothing auto-publishes without a human veto window.
Failure-with-a-fix: a GitHub push stalled when the auth token scope failed; we recovered by delegating the auth/push out of the command thread and the open-source repo still shipped live.
Product pivot worth telling: mid-build we reframed Wingbeat's surface from a plain 'skill' into a Hermes plugin others can install — a small decision that changed the whole distribution story.
opportunities.evidenceQuote: build this repo as a plugin to be installed by hermes

opportunities.description: Orchestration lesson for solo builders: keeping one 'chief of staff' thread that only delegates to worktree agents (never codes itself) is how we ran many parallel agents without losing the plot.
opportunities.evidenceQuote: you should always delegate to gpt 5.5 worktree threads
